task_completion.description: The source image is opened in an image editing application (GIMP).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the GIMP application window containing a standard photograph (continuous tones, no halftone effect yet) loaded onto the main canvas.

task_completion.description: The Filters menu is accessed in the menu bar.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Filters' menu dropped down from the top menu bar of the application.
Look for the 'Distorts' option highlighted in the Filters menu, or its submenu expanded.

task_completion.description: The Newsprint filter dialog is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a popup window titled 'Newsprint' containing settings for the halftone effect.

task_completion.description: The newsprint/halftone effect is visible in the preview or on the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the image changing to a halftone pattern (visible dots, lines, or diamonds making up the image) either on the main canvas or within a preview thumbnail in the dialog box.

task_completion.description: The newsprint effect is finalized on the main image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the final image on the main canvas fully rendered with the newsprint/halftone effect with the dialog box closed, or an export/save dialog indicating the modified image is being saved.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of an image editor to apply the effect.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show mouse clicks, menu navigation, and GUI interaction in GIMP, rather than terminal commands (like ImageMagick) being typed.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with a real image editor rather than a script or text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window must be clearly visible with its standard toolbars, docks, and menus.

integrity.description: The agent applied the effect to the provided image rather than opening a pre-existing halftone image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression from the original continuous-tone photograph to the halftone version must be visible across the sequence of screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires applying GIMP's Newsprint filter (typically found under Filters -> Distorts -> Newsprint) to a downloaded photograph to create a halftone effect.
